title: Streaming run progress (in-place edits) + silent scheduled digest delivery
description: Add two outbound delivery modes over the sendSafe funnel: (a) streaming — a long-running operation reports progress by editing one status message in place as progress events arrive, ending with a final summary; (b) silent digest — a scheduler-handed digest payload is sent with notifications disabled. No run, fleet, or scheduler logic is added; this remains a presentation adapter.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the streaming and digest delivery additions with zero TypeScript errors.

A unit test shows the streaming sender, given a sequence of N>=2 progress updates, issues one send call and at least two edit calls on the same message id (verified via an injected transport spy) rather than one new message per update.
A unit test shows the digest sender sets the disable_notification flag on the outgoing message.
A unit test shows both the streaming and digest senders route their content through the sendSafe funnel.
Triggering a long do-bridge run shows a status message in Telegram that updates in place at least twice and ends with a final result; a delivered digest arrives without a notification sound.

nonGoals: Do not implement the scheduler itself — this sprint only delivers a digest payload handed to it; scheduling cadence is owned elsewhere.
Do not add run or fleet execution logic — read existing run progress signals only; this stays a presentation adapter.
Do not send sensitive run detail — status updates and digests carry summaries only via sendSafe.
Do not post a new message per progress tick — edit a single status message in place.
Do not bypass the whitelist or the sendSafe funnel.
stopConditions: The streaming (one send, multiple edits) and silent-flag unit tests pass and `npm run build` exits zero.
A digest send sets disable_notification and a streamed run produces in-place edits rather than repeated new messages.
definitionOfDone: Long-running operations report progress by editing one Telegram status message in place as progress events arrive, ending with a final summary, instead of posting a new message per tick. Scheduled digests are delivered with notifications silenced (disable_notification). Both paths emit only non-sensitive summaries through the sendSafe funnel, and neither adds run, fleet, or scheduler logic.
assumptions: Run progress is read from existing signals (e.g. history.jsonl events / telemetry) without adding run logic; the streaming sender consumes an async iterable / callback of progress updates supplied by the caller.
The Telegram transport wrapper from Sprint 1 exposes editMessage and a disable_notification option on sendMessage (libraries supporting getUpdates expose both).
A 'digest payload' is plain summary text handed in by the scheduler owner; this adapter only sends it silently.
outOfScope: Deciding what goes into a digest or when it fires (scheduler, owned elsewhere).
Token-level streaming from the model — progress is event/step granularity, not per-token.
generatorNotes: Add src/telegram/streaming.ts (streamProgress(transport, chatId, updates): send an initial status message, then call editMessage on that message id for each subsequent update, final edit = summary) and src/telegram/digest.ts (sendDigest(transport, chatId, text): send via sendSafe with disable_notification: true). Both go through the sendSafe funnel from Sprint 1 (extend the funnel to accept delivery options like silent/edit-target rather than calling the transport directly). Source progress generically: accept an injected async iterable or onUpdate callback so the unit test drives a fixed sequence; for the manual path, tail existing run progress (history.jsonl/telemetry) — do not add run logic. Wire so a do-bridge promotion run (resolved via Sprint 4's gate) reports via streamProgress. ESM .js imports, `import type`, no `any`, node:fs/promises only.
evaluatorNotes: With an injected transport spy, drive >=2 progress updates and assert exactly one send + >=2 edits on the same message id. Assert sendDigest sets disable_notification true. Assert both paths call sendSafe (no direct transport send). Confirm no run/fleet/scheduler logic was added (diff stays within src/telegram/). Run build + the streaming/digest unit tests.
